answers: Body language is nonverbal communication through gestures, posture, facial expressions, and eye contact. Studies show over 60% of communication is nonverbal.
Confident body language include kore: straight posture, steady eye contact, firm handshake, open arms (crossed na), ar relaxed shoulders. Ei small changes boro impact create kore.
To read body language, look for clusters of signals: crossed arms might mean discomfort, leaning in shows interest, avoiding eye contact could mean nervousness, and genuine smiles reach the eyes.

Improve your body language by practicing good posture, making appropriate eye contact, using open gestures, and mirroring the other person subtly. Record yourself speaking to spot habits you want to change.
